About the job
Culturally, this agency exudes genuine warmth, capacious intelligence, and a passion for real human insight that can shift behaviours and reshape our world for the better.

Hard to find elsewhere, the team thrives on mixed-methodology projects - seamlessly weaving quantitative and qualitative approaches to tackle both social and commercial research challenges.

Their super-loyal team stays for the purposeful social impact work (often in social marketing and behavioural change), alongside wickedly sharp commercial challenges across brand, innovation, and communications.

They offer exceptional learning and development to help you grow your career as a Strategic Insights Consultant.

Key responsibilities:

Confidently build strong client relationships
Support the delivery of complex research programs - from design through to insight communication
Further develop skills in survey design, meticulous project management, statistical analysis, reporting, and presenting with strategic perspective
Get involved in qualitative research - including design, depth interviews, and focus groups
Leverage insights from alternative sources, including their fabulous trends monitor
Bring curiosity, energy, and ideas to support a brilliant team

What you’re looking for:

Variety in methodologies - qualitative and quantitative
Diversity of clients - Government, NFPs, banks, retailers, tourism, and energy
Meaningful human insights work, such as: Designing road safety campaigns; Behaviour change programs for marine safety; Commercialising green energy; Innovation and choice modelling within insurance; Identifying opportunities to drive summer tourism in ski resorts; Understanding behaviour and demand at cemeteries. 
A renowned agency with an outstanding reputation
Part of a creative group, offering a strong network to leverage
Epic offices, rooftop bar, vibrant culture, socials, hybrid working, additional leave, and learning opportunities

What we’re looking for:

Experience within another social or market research agency is essential
Experience in a customised research role with exposure to the full research cycle
Experience managing multiple research projects and switching between tasks
Strong analytical, interpretive, and storytelling skills
Experience with a statistical package such as Q or SPSS
Natural confidence in a client-facing role
Tertiary qualifications in marketing, social sciences, psychology, business, or a related field

Send your CV to Senior Partner Gemma Lewis at glewis@resourcesgroup.com or call 0452 417 265 for a confidential discussion about this role and other opportunities.

About the job
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Get to Know our Team:

The Performance Marketing Team of Agoda is a world leader in online marketing. This department is highly data-driven and focused on developing at-scale marketing programs that improve the lifetime value of Agoda customers through measurable marketing programs and channels. The team is a blend of the best analysts, marketing strategists, and data scientists in the world. The marketing leadership at Agoda have deep experience in data science, product, strategy, and other marketing fields and have built an organization that thrives on data, creative ideas, and technology. The Performance Marketing Team also fosters a great learning environment. You will be able to learn and grow by working closely with experts from a variety of backgrounds from all over the world.

In this Role, you’ll get to:

Search: Experiment with text ads, bidding, and campaign structures on Google, Bing, Baidu, Naver, and other search engines. Adapt to new product features and roll out changes from successful tests.
Display: Test, analyze, and optimize campaigns on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and others.
Modeling: Analyze the vast amounts of data generated by experiments, develop models we can use for optimization, and build dashboards for account managers.

What you’ll Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s Degree or higher from top university in a quantitative subject (computer science, mathematics, economics, engineering, statistics or science)
Fresh graduation are welcome (0-1 years of experience)
Ability to communicate fluently in English
Basic business acumen
Proficient in statistics and Excel
Exposure to data tools (SAS, R, SPSS, Python, VBA, SQL, Tableau)
Familiar with A/B testing concepts
Works under close supervision and supports projects with guidance

It’s Great if you Have:

Experience in digital marketing
Academic research experience
